title: clematis spathulifolia
intro: Clematis spathulifolia, also known as the Leather Flower, is a climbing perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant's roots are known for their medicinal properties, particularly for their antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Clematis spathulifolia include treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections, as well as having antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal use.
